title: Pilot intake connectors and durable case lifecycle
description: Turn the current bounded intake flow into a pilot-usable case lifecycle. Add one or two real intake entry shapes, preserve durable case state beyond the current demo loop, and make support cases feel like managed work items rather than only demo incidents.
implementation_tasks: define a pilot-safe intake model for at least two real entry shapes such as webhook-style payloads and structured pasted incident reports
persist case lifecycle fields that matter for pilot use: created, triaged, handed off, approved, executed, and closed
tighten queue and history surfaces so pilot cases read as durable work items rather than only flagship demos
document which intake paths are truly supported in the pilot versus still demo-only
done_when: NEXUS can accept more than one realistic intake path for the pilot workflow
case lifecycle state persists cleanly across queue, incident, and history surfaces
pilot operators can understand where a case is in its lifecycle without relying on founder narration

title: Real downstream handoff actions for engineering workflows
description: Move past export-only artifacts and support at least one real downstream handoff path. The operator should be able to send a bounded engineering-ready packet into a real workflow target such as GitHub, Jira, or Slack while preserving truth labels and governance context.
implementation_tasks: choose one primary downstream target and one secondary target for real pilot delivery
implement a bounded send action that uses existing handoff packet content instead of creating a second inconsistent artifact path
preserve runtime-backed versus inferred evidence labels and governance posture in the downstream payload
record delivery status and target metadata back into the NEXUS case record
done_when: operators can send at least one engineering handoff into a real downstream workflow from the incident console
delivery status is visible in the case lifecycle after the send action completes or fails
the downstream payload preserves the same truth semantics shown in the product

title: Tenant-specific ownership and repository mapping
description: Replace more of the generic flagship ownership story with tenant-aware mappings. The product should be able to resolve likely owners, repository paths, and service boundaries from customer-specific configuration rather than leaning mostly on checked-in demo maps.
implementation_tasks: define a tenant-aware mapping substrate for service ownership, escalation destination, and repo association
feed that mapping into triage summary, TRACE ownership cues, and engineering handoff packaging
preserve safe fallback behavior when tenant mappings are incomplete
make the UI explicit about whether an owner or repo cue came from tenant configuration or bounded fallback logic
done_when: ownership and repo cues can come from tenant-specific configuration for pilot cases
TRACE and handoff packets visibly distinguish tenant-mapped cues from fallback cues
operators get more believable owner-routing and engineering targeting in the pilot flow

title: Engineering feedback loop and outcome ingestion
description: Make the support-to-engineering handoff bidirectional. Once engineering accepts, edits, rejects, or resolves a handoff, that feedback should come back into NEXUS so memory, outcome history, and ROI reflect what happened in the real workflow.
implementation_tasks: define a bounded feedback model for accepted, edited, rejected, resolved, and reopened engineering outcomes
ingest the feedback into case history, execution outcome, and memory ranking
make outcome provenance visible so operators know whether the result came from internal execution, engineering confirmation, or both
keep seeded and live stories aligned by adding honest fallback behavior when no external feedback exists
done_when: engineering workflow outcomes can be captured back into NEXUS
memory and case history reflect real downstream results rather than only internal assumptions
operators can see whether a suggested action was accepted, changed, or rejected by engineering

title: Admin controls for packs, policies, and destinations
description: Introduce bounded operator administration so pilot teams do not need code edits for every environment change. Enabled runtime packs, debugger support, approval policies, and delivery targets should be visible and manageable through product-facing controls or configuration surfaces.
implementation_tasks: define a bounded admin model for enabled packs, approval policies, and delivery destinations
surface current configuration in a product-facing settings or training-adjacent view
support safe disablement when a pack or downstream target should not be used for a tenant
document clearly which settings are product-managed versus still code-managed
done_when: pilot operators can inspect active packs, policies, and destinations without reading code
at least some runtime and handoff controls can be changed without editing source files directly
the product feels closer to an administrable workflow system rather than a fixed demo

title: Operator ROI and buyer-value metrics v2
description: Make the buyer case measurable. NEXUS should show not just workflow stages, but business-relevant metrics such as relay steps reduced, triage time saved, replay reuse, approval turnaround, and engineering handoff conversion so support leaders can evaluate the product as a cost and speed lever.
implementation_tasks: define a bounded ROI metric model tied to support relay reduction and faster decision-making
compute metrics from case history, replay usage, handoff sends, approvals, and downstream outcomes
surface the metrics in an operator- and buyer-readable view
keep the numbers honest by labeling estimated versus measured values
done_when: the product shows buyer-relevant workflow metrics rather than only technical activity summaries
operators can explain how NEXUS reduced relay work or time-to-action on the flagship workflow
estimated versus measured metrics are clearly distinguished

title: One repo-aware debugger handoff for the pilot environment
description: Upgrade the bounded debugger story from generic curated-pack guidance to one real pilot environment handoff. For one outage class and one repository mapping, the debugger packet should point to a believable owner, file path, and inspection path that an engineering team could act on directly.
implementation_tasks: choose one pilot outage class and one repository mapping as the first real debugger target
map TRACE output to tenant-aware file ownership and inspection cues rather than generic pack-only references
preserve bounded-language and unsupported-state handling for all non-pilot cases
tighten the engineering handoff export so the repo-aware debugger packet is the default path for the chosen pilot class
done_when: one outage class has a repo-aware debugger handoff grounded in tenant mapping rather than only demo paths
engineering can use the packet as a realistic first debugging brief
all other cases remain honestly bounded instead of inheriting unsupported claims

title: One tenant-specific runtime pack upgrade for the pilot environment
description: Deepen REPLICA for one real pilot environment. Instead of broadening to many more packs, upgrade one flagship runtime pack to reflect one tenant's real stack or operating conditions closely enough that reproduction becomes materially more convincing for the pilot workflow.
implementation_tasks: choose one flagship outage and define the tenant-specific runtime differences that matter for replay credibility
upgrade one existing curated pack or add one bounded variant tied to the pilot environment
preserve generic curated-pack behavior for non-pilot usage while making the pilot upgrade explicit
measure and expose why the tenant-specific pack is closer to reality than the generic pack
done_when: one flagship runtime pack is upgraded with tenant-specific realism for pilot use
operators can see when the pilot pack is in use versus the generic curated pack
the reproduction story is materially stronger for one real environment without broadening unsupported scope
